So I wanted to take a second to explain to you why we want to reach 2,000 donors. It's because we want to show climate change deniers and big oil special interests what a huge force they are up against this session. We want to show them that we are united -- and they can't bring us down. Subject: I don't know how to make this more clear

[](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7ad7b/2f5ea88d/7a4153d6/2cbdde73/1858546971/VEsO/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ji-fr-freeze-1214?utm_source=ema_2018-12-13_freeze-5-kicker&refcode=ema_2018-12-13_freeze-5-kicker&utm_campaign=freeze-5-kicker&utm_medium=ema")
Friend,
I don't know how to make this any more clear -- this legally-mandated fundraising freeze deadline is a make or break moment for our movement. Here's why I need your help.
Just this week, global investors gave us a dire warning: If we don't drastically reduce carbon emissions and completely halt burning coal, we will face a global financial crash. **Look, we've known the threat of climate change for a long time.** But we also know how hard the fossil fuel special interests will fight tooth and nail against climate action.
So here's the thing -- starting at midnight tomorrow, I won't be able to raise funds due to a state-mandated fundraising freeze. During the legislative session, I'll be fighting my hardest to push forward an agenda that provides real, clear solutions to climate change. But I need your help today to make sure we can keep our movement moving forward and fighting for progress during the legislative session. **That's why I'm asking you to become one of the 2,000 donors we need to reach our bold goal before m idnight tomorrow.**
[**Will you help us hit our goal and become a pre-freeze donor to show your support right now?**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7ad7b/2f5ea88d/7a4153d6/2cbdde73/1858546971/VEsP/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ji-fr-freeze-1214?utm_source=ema_2018-12-13_freeze-5-kicker&refcode=ema_2018-12-13_freeze-5-kicker&utm_campaign=freeze-5-kicker&utm_medium=ema")
[](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7ad7b/2f5ea88d/7a4153d6/2cbdde73/1858546971/VEsHBQ/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ji-fr-freeze-1214?utm_source=ema_2018-12-13_freeze-5-kicker&refcode=ema_2018-12-13_freeze-5-kicker&utm_campaign=freeze-5-kicker&utm_medium=ema")
This week, I announced the boldest state-based climate agenda in the nation. Period. My plan includes a commitment to reach a 100% clean energy future so that by 2030, power utilities in our state will be fully carbon neutral. I run the state with the strongest economy in the country, so I know full-well that my agenda isn't just about saving our planet, it's also about creating jobs. So while plenty of politicians -- especially in D.C. -- love to talk about climate change, I'm going to use my role as governor to take real action.
But I can't do it without you. I need your help today to keep our grassroots movement strong during this freeze and I need to do it now. Join me: [**https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ji-fr-freeze-1214**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7ad7b/2f5ea88d/7a4153d6/2cbdde73/1858546971/VEsHBA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ji-fr-freeze-1214?utm_source=ema_2018-12-13_freeze-5-kicker&refcode=ema_2018-12-13_freeze-5-kicker&utm_campaign=freeze-5-kicker&utm_medium=ema")
I hope you know how serious I am about ending our dependence on fossil fuels forever. But I need your people power behind me to show them that we can -- and will -- accomplish it.
Very truly yours,
Jay Inslee

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: The First Family’s busy holiday season
body:
As Christmas approaches, President Donald J. Trump and the First Family have been hard at work to ensure every American has a wonderful holiday...

https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0c72730bd0&e=3ee4985eea
The White House • December 13, 2018

** A busy holiday season at the White House
------------------------------------------------------------
As Christmas approaches, President Donald J. Trump and the First Family have been hard at work to ensure every American has a wonderful holiday season.

On the policy side, the President has focused on delivering several crucial wins before the calendar turns to 2019:
* On Tuesday, he met with Democratic leaders (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9bb28b13e5&e=3ee4985eea) in the Oval Office to make clear border security is nonnegotiable when it comes to funding our Government.

* Yesterday, he signed an “Opportunity Zones” executive order that will help drive investment into the most underserved communities (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b5e33a8544&e=3ee4985eea) in America.

* This week, Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell (R-KY) vowed to bring the First Step Act—historic criminal justice reform (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a2039539d3&e=3ee4985eea) that President Trump is backing strongly—to the Senate floor.

The Christmas season is also about supporting our fellow Americans.

The First Lady kicked off the week by joining the U.S. Marine Corps Reserve for its annual Toys for Tots drive in Washington. “As a mother, I believe children are the most precious gift,” she said, donating 100 books for the Toys for Tots Literacy Program—a project that dovetails with the First Lady’s own “Be Best” initiative for children. (See photos from the event (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b9eb41a15b&e=3ee4985eea) .)

Yesterday, the First Lady honored our heroes in uniform—many of whom will be serving our country over the Christmas season. She made stops at Joint Base Anacostia-Bolling, Joint Base Langley-Eustis, and the USS George H.W. Bush aircraft carrier. She also flew in a V-22 Osprey military plane, becoming the first First Lady ever to do so.

And today, the First Lady traveled to Children’s National Hospital in Washington. The visit is a special tradition that’s been followed by every First Lady since Bess Truman more than 60 years ago.

Watch highlights from the First Lady’s recent visit to the Red Cross (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=839189c00d&e=3ee4985eea) .

A special tribute: See how the First Lady honored the USS. George H.W. Bush (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ad3c0c8b10&e=3ee4985eea)

** America’s new Governors have a partner in the White House
------------------------------------------------------------
President Trump, Vice President Mike Pence, and senior members of the Trump Administration welcomed a bipartisan group of America’s 13 newest Governors to the White House this afternoon.

Government works best when our elected officials focus on shared priorities—regardless of party label. By forging relationships with State leaders, President Trump and his Administration can tackle the issues that matter most to working Americans, including job training, support for veterans, and the war on opioid abuse.

“Congratulations to each of you. And consider us a resource and a partner in working with you on some issues of the day, like workforce development, prison reform, opioids and the fentanyl crisis, and obviously all things economic," Counselor to the President Kellyanne Conway said.

What the Governors-elect told President Trump at the White House today. (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=928d2ba9cb&e=3ee4985eea)

Inside look: See the full group meet with the President in the Cabinet Room (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d2ac0549d6&e=3ee4985eea)

** Photo of the Day
------------------------------------------------------------
Official White House Photo by Andrea Hanks
First Lady Melania Trump observes flight operations with U.S. Navy Capt. Chris Hill during a holiday visit with sailors aboard USS George H.W. Bush | December 12, 2018

Privacy Policy (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2578bcf2b9&e=3ee4985eea) | Contact the White House (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6e76e9c961&e=3ee4985eea) | Unsubscribe (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=8bacaa3f02)

climate change NOW.

Last month, a federal report was released detailing the impending
damage of climate change. If we fail to act, natural disasters
including wildfires, hurricanes, droughts, and more will only get
worse.

your name to join me in demanding immediate climate action from

Friend,

There are a ton of organizations out there wishing the President a Merry Christmas.

**But this is the _ONLY_ Official First Family Christmas Card.** And as one of the President's top supporters, we need YOU to sign it.

Our wonderful First Lady has been working so hard to decorate the White House for this joyous time of year.

She and the President know that the Christmas spirit is an integral part of our country's traditions.

We hope that you'll join in on the Christmas festivities led by the President and Melania by signing the Official First Family Christmas Card.

name: U.S. Senator Bernie Sanders
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Bernie Sanders
body:
For the first time in the history of the United States
#

Sisters and Brothers -
Yesterday afternoon, for the first time in the history of the United States, the U.S. Senate passed a resolution I sponsored along with Mike Lee of Utah and Chris Murphy of Connecticut, withdrawing the United States Armed Forces from an unauthorized and unconstitutional war.
The margin was 56 to 41 in support of ending our involvement in the war in Yemen. That is, in my view, a number of “yes” votes that most people would have believed unthinkable even just a few weeks ago.
I’ve written to you a lot about my work to put an end to this moral and humanitarian catastrophe, so today I want to update you on what happens next, and then I am going to ask you to take action in support of that work.
In order for this bill to get to President Trump’s desk, it must pass the House of Representatives. Unfortunately, Speaker Paul Ryan has effectively disallowed a vote on the resolution, despite the fact it would pass with bipartisan support.
So either something has to change, or the new Congress under Nancy Pelosi’s leadership will have to take it up again in early 2019. Meanwhile, many Yemeni children and families will die. More will suffer until we finally act and force these two sides to come to a resolution that ends this moral abomination.
My hope is that if we can put enough pressure on Paul Ryan and the Republicans in Congress, something can change. And if it doesn’t, the new Congress will know without question that the American people no longer want to play a part in the death and destruction in Yemen.
**So I am asking you to make your voice heard:**
****[Sign my petition: tell Speaker Ryan and the House of Representatives to vote on legislation that would end the United States’ support for the Saudi-led war in Yemen.](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/house_yemen_bill/?source=em181214-full&t=1&akid=385%2E150750%2EWBgSJL)****
[SIGN THE PETITION](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/house_yemen_bill/?source=em181214-full&t=2&akid=385%2E150750%2EWBgSJL)
Here is the truth: by continuing to back Saudi Arabia’s war in Yemen with weapons, aerial refueling, and targeting assistance, the United States has been complicit in the worst humanitarian disaster on Earth — a war which no one can seriously claim is actually making us safer.
Instead, 85,000 children have starved to death, 14 million more people are on the brink of starvation, and the country is facing the worst cholera outbreak in the world. According to the United Nations, the war in Yemen has created the most severe famine in more than 100 years.
And unless we use the power of this country NOT to drop more bombs and instead to bring the warring parties together, this situation will become even worse.
The good news is, through our action in the Senate, we are already placing enormous amounts of pressure on Saudi Arabia — pressure that has already resulted in some breakthroughs in the peace negotiations.
But we must do more: The House must pass this legislation, President Trump must sign it, and we must end our involvement in a war that was never authorized, and is therefore unconstitutional.
**Add your name if you agree:**
****[Sign my petition: tell Speaker Ryan and the House of Representatives to vote on legislation that would end the United States’ support for the Saudi-led war in Yemen.](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/house_yemen_bill/?source=em181214-full&t=3&akid=385%2E150750%2EWBgSJL)****
The United States is a country that has stood for democracy and human rights throughout our existence. We should not be partnering with a country responsible for murdering a dissident in cold blood and that is leading an effort causing mass starvation and the worst humanitarian disaster on Earth.
Yesterday we took one step closer toward that goal. And that is only possible because people all across the country are waking up to our involvement in Yemen and making their voices heard.
In solidarity,
Bernie Sanders

* * *

[SIGN THE PETITION](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/house_yemen_bill/?source=em181214-full&t=4&akid=385%2E150750%2EWBgSJL)
Paid for by Friends of Bernie Sanders
PO BOX 391, Burlington, VT 05402
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is one of the most important tools we have to reach supporters like you. We'd hate to see you go, but if you need to do so, [click here to unsubscribe](https://bernie.actionkit.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=5&akid=385%2E150750%2EWBgSJL). To support Bernie with a contribution, [click here](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-em-footer?refcode=em-footer&t=6&akid=385%2E150750%2EWBgSJL).

---|---|---
|

Friend,

Thank you for all that you do. As a fellow Hoosier, it means the world that you’re spending the time to keep up-to-date with our team’s work to take Indiana to the Next Level.

**And there are a lot of great things in the works for Indiana!**

From major new infrastructure projects, to our efforts to fight the drug epidemic, grow our workforce, and attract more high-wage jobs to the state (_all while maintaining fiscal responsibility_), we’d like to make sure we keep you updated on the latest news in Indiana.

I’d encourage you to keep an eye on your inbox for updates. We’ll share important news, and most importantly, we’ll gather your opinions on the things that matter most. So, stay tuned!

But for now, I hope you’ll LIKE us on Facebook and follow us on Twitter for real time updates!

subject: First Lady Makes Annual Christmas Visit to Children’s National Hospital
body:
"Melania Trump spread her anti-bullying message on an annual Christmas season visit to a Washington children’s hospital..." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5fddbc7d55&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **First Lady Spreads Anti-Bullying Message at Kids’ Hospital**

“Melania Trump spread her anti-bullying message on an annual Christmas season visit to a Washington children’s hospital on Thursday, reading a story about a Christmas ornament named Oliver who is bullied by other ornaments in a family’s collection,” Darlene Superville writes for _The Associated Press._

The author of the children’s book sat a few feet away, thanking the First Lady “from the bottom of my heart” for inviting him to join her. “Mrs. Trump launched an initiative earlier this year to teach kindness to children, naming it Be Best,” Superville adds.

[Click here to continue reading.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=1a5ed8d579&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “The number of Americans applying for unemployment benefits plunged by 27,000 in early December to 206,000, erasing a surge around Thanksgiving that now appears to have been a temporary blip triggered by an earlier-than-usual holiday,” Jeffry Bartash reports for _[MarketWatch](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=08e63eb452&e=3ee4985eea)_. “The decline in applications for benefits puts claims, a rough way to measure layoffs, back near a half-century low.”
---
|
---
| “Top Trump adviser Jared Kushner is winning bipartisan praise for driving a 20-year effort to reform prison sentencing and criminal justice to the finish line and ignoring repeated declarations that it was dead right up to last week,” Paul Bedard writes in the _[Washington Examiner](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5fdaad11f3&e=3ee4985eea)_. Both liberal and conservative prison reform advocates are giving Kushner credit for uniting “an unlikely coalition that has the package on the verge of Senate passage.”
---
|
---
| “Congress must fund President Donald Trump’s border wall and close the ‘catch and release’ loopholes in the upcoming must-pass spending bill. We must do it now.” Rep. Ted Budd (R-NC) writes in _[The Daily Signal](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=92903c6305&e=3ee4985eea)_. “The problem of illegal immigration is solvable, but will require compromise. A good place to start will be with border security measures that Democrats have historically agreed to.”
---
|
---
| “National security adviser John Bolton unveiled the Trump administration's new strategy for Africa as it works to counter Russia and China, as they expand their financial, political and military investments across the continent,” Elizabeth McLaughlin reports for [ABC News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4f16c62944&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|

I 'm holding a town hall on Facebook Live and I would be honored if you'd join the conversation.** | | | [RSVP](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffdc9/4462dc26/89181d6f/201ef9e8/2528583102/VEsH/ "https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/e181214d")

I'm excited to hear any questions, comments, or ideas you have -- so if you have one, you can send it in ahead of time. [**Just head over to our Facebook event page to RSVP.**](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffdc9/4462dc26/89181d6f/201ef9e8/2528583102/VEsE/ "https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/e181214d")
I plan on talking about what we're fighting for in the months ahead:
* Protecting the dignity of work and giving families a strong voice in Washington.
* Preserving Social Security, Medicare, and the Affordable Care Act.
* Ending _Citizens United_ and bringing transparency to our elections.

And of course, I'll be answering your questions and hearing your ideas, too. I'm looking forward to hearing what you have to say!
[**Click here to RSVP to our Facebook Live town hall this Sunday, December 16, at 1pm PST/4pm EST.**](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffdc9/4462dc26/89181d6f/201ef9e8/2528583102/VEsF/ "https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/e181214d")

Sherrod

subject: Get Covered! (Deadline TOMORROW)
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tim Ryan for Congress
body:
Enrollment is open to purchase 2019 health insurance coverage through the Affordable Care Act's exchange. Tomorrow, December 15th, is the deadline to purchase 2019 coverage. | |
---
Important reminder, folks: Enrollment is open to purchase 2019 health insurance coverage through the Affordable Care Act's exchange. **Tomorrow, December 15th, is the deadline to purchase 2019 coverage. **[You can register online here >>](http://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/m/d7a90e9/5486deaa/25842498/dcf505b/2640268525/VEsH/ "https://www.healthcare.gov/") [GET COVERED](http://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/m/d7a90e9/5486deaa/25842498/dcf505b/2640268525/VEsE/ "https://www.healthcare.gov/") (Already covered? Help spread the word by sharing on [Facebook](http://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/m/d7a90e9/5486deaa/25842498/dcf505a/2640268525/VEsF/ "https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.healthcare.gov/") and [Twitter](http://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/m/d7a90e9/5486deaa/25842498/dcf505d/2640268525/VEsC/ "https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?text=The%20deadline%20to%20enroll%20in%202019%20health%20coverage%20via%20HealthCare.gov%20is%20SATURDAY%2C%20DECEMBER%2015.%20%0D%0A%0D%0A%23GetCovered%20today%3A%20http%3A%2F%2Fhealthcare.gov%0D%0A%0D%0A%28via%20%40TimRyan%29%0D%0Ahttps%3A%2F%2Ftwitter.com%2FBarackObama%2Fstatus%2F1072154184382844928&source=clicktotweet&related=clicktotweet")!) In the wealthiest country in the world, every single person should be able to see a doctor without worrying about what's in their wallet. That's why Tim has supported Medicare for All for over a decade and is a proud founding member of the Medicare for All Caucus in the House. **The Affordable Care Act made it possible for millions more Americans to access the care that they need -- and we're fighting tooth-and-nail to protect that law from repeal or sabotage.** Friends, we want to make sure that you have the chance to register and receive the care you and your family need in 2019. [Make sure that you sign up before the December 15th deadline to get covered!](http://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/m/d7a90e9/5486deaa/25842498/dcf505b/2640268525/VEsD/ "https://www.healthcare.gov/") (And if you're already covered, help spread the word about the upcoming deadline by sharing on [Facebook](http://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/m/d7a90e9/5486deaa/25842498/dcf505a/2640268525/VEsA/ "https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.healthcare.gov/") and [Twitter](http://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/m/d7a90e9/5486deaa/25842498/dcf505d/2640268525/VEsB/ "https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?text=The%20deadline%20to%20enroll%20in%202019%20health%20coverage%20via%20HealthCare.gov%20is%20SATURDAY%2C%20DECEMBER%2015.%20%0D%0A%0D%0A%23GetCovered%20today%3A%20http%3A%2F%2Fhealthcare.gov%0D%0A%0D%0A%28via%20%40TimRyan%29%0D%0Ahttps%3A%2F%2Ftwitter.com%2FBarackObama%2Fstatus%2F1072154184382844928&source=clicktotweet&related=clicktotweet").) Thank you, \-- Team Ryan

subject: People, people, people
body:
We've all heard Governor Holcomb say it. For him, his job is all about "people, people, people." And a quick look at his 2019 Next Level Agenda... | | | [View this email in your browser](https://us11.campaign-archive.com/?e=1acd9bb3d6&u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=5f4fb93321)
---
| |
---
|
---
| **_People, People, People_** We've all heard **Governor Holcomb** say it. For him, his job is all about "people, people, people." And a quick look at his 2019 Next Level Agenda ([which you can read here](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=5898c5ca07&e=1acd9bb3d6)) shows that he's continuing to place Hoosiers front-and-center among his top priorities. He's squarely-focused on policies that will lift all Hoosiers.

Reading through his agenda, we've got the standard items in there. We'll pass Indiana's eighth-straight honestly-balanced budget, and we'll work to keep Indiana the fiscal envy of the nation.

But he's also got some great ideas in there that will truly improve lives. Here's just a few:
* Exempting military pensions from state income tax (helping us attract and retain more vets here)
* Expanding high-speed broadband access to unserved areas
* Increasing the school supplies tax credit for teachers
* Working toward increasing teacher salaries
* Expanding the popular grants that are allowing Hoosiers to go back to school to gain high-wage job skills
* Implementing school safety recommendations
* Establishing a program that will help lower the infant mortality rate
* Helping people recover from the opioid epidemic.

And that's just the beginning. Governor Holcomb is working hard for us, and with his leadership at the helm, I can’t wait to see what 2019 holds for Indiana.
-Kyle Hupfer

_**Photo:** Chairman Kyle Hupfer joined the Jewish Lawyers Luncheon Group on Wednesday this week to provide an insider's view on the 2018 election and to discuss what the election results mean for the future of Indiana and our nation. If you would be interested in having Chairman Hupfer speak to your group in 2019, please email us at [info@indiana.gop](mailto:info@indiana.gop)._
---
|
---
| | | **GOVERNOR HOLCOMB'S WEEK THAT WAS**
---
|
---
---
| **_Jobs for Indiana's Birthday_** Happy birthday, Indiana! Tuesday, December 11, was Statehood Day in Indiana, which celebrated 202 years since Indiana became the 19th state to join the union. As one of Indiana’s top history buffs, **Governor Eric Holcomb** was on hand at the Statehouse with hundreds of fourth graders to celebrate our state’s birthday and all that we’ve accomplished as Hoosiers in the last 202 years.

“This has always been my favorite celebration in this stretch of our year,” said **Governor Holcomb**. [Read more](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=5d7af96b5a&e=1acd9bb3d6) about Statehood Day, and how**Governor Holcomb** helped celebrate this important day for Indiana.

Then **Governor Holcomb** traveled north, where he joined California-based energy company Fulcrum BioEnergy as the company announced [big plans to establish operations in the state](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=49fb0f7622&e=1acd9bb3d6) – including 163 new, high-wage jobs in Gary! This is fantastic news, and truly a great birthday present for Indiana!
---
|
---
| | **MIKE BRAUN CORNER**
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=a344b57861&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| **_Meet With Senator-elect Mike Braun_** There’s still a few weeks until**Senator-elect Mike Braun** officially takes office, but he’s already working for us. He’s holding office hours next week, which will provide Hoosiers with a chance to meet their new senator and discuss issues that matter most to them. And he’d love to hear from you!

clean_body: We've all heard Governor Holcomb say it. For him, his job is all about "people, people, people." And a quick look at his 2019 Next Level Agenda... | | | | | | | People, People, People We've all heard Governor Holcomb say it. For him, his job is all about "people, people, people." And a quick look at his 2019 Next Level Agenda () shows that he's continuing to place Hoosiers front-and-center among his top priorities. He's squarely-focused on policies that will lift all Hoosiers. Reading through his agenda, we've got the standard items in there. We'll pass Indiana's eighth-straight honestly-balanced budget, and we'll work to keep Indiana the fiscal envy of the nation. But he's also got some great ideas in there that will truly improve lives. Here's just a few: Exempting military pensions from state income tax (helping us attract and retain more vets here) Expanding high-speed broadband access to unserved areas Increasing the school supplies tax credit for teachers Working toward increasing teacher salaries Expanding the popular grants that are allowing Hoosiers to go back to school to gain high-wage job skills Implementing school safety recommendations Establishing a program that will help lower the infant mortality rate Helping people recover from the opioid epidemic. And that's just the beginning. Governor Holcomb is working hard for us, and with his leadership at the helm, I can’t wait to see what 2019 holds for Indiana. -Kyle Hupfer Photo: Chairman Kyle Hupfer joined the Jewish Lawyers Luncheon Group on Wednesday this week to provide an insider's view on the 2018 election and to discuss what the election results mean for the future of Indiana and our nation. If you would be interested in having Chairman Hupfer speak to your group in 2019, please email us at mailto:info@indiana.gop. | | | | GOVERNOR HOLCOMB'S WEEK THAT WAS | | Jobs for Indiana's Birthday Happy birthday, Indiana! Tuesday, December 11, was Statehood Day in Indiana, which celebrated 202 years since Indiana became the 19th state to join the union. As one of Indiana’s top history buffs, Governor Eric Holcomb was on hand at the Statehouse with hundreds of fourth graders to celebrate our state’s birthday and all that we’ve accomplished as Hoosiers in the last 202 years. “This has always been my favorite celebration in this stretch of our year,” said Governor Holcomb. about Statehood Day, and howGovernor Holcomb helped celebrate this important day for Indiana.
